summary: ZemDev MVP first-pass testing complete. Backend 10/10 pytest passed (auth login/logout/me, products list/get/CRUD with admin auth, 401/404 paths). Frontend landing page renders ZemDev hero, 6 product cards, working category filters (Scripts/Bases/MLOs/Vehículos), purchase modal opens with BBVA bank info + Discord button, admin login flow works (valid creds redirect to /admin), admin dashboard shows products table and 'Nuevo Producto' modal opens correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py CORS: allow_origins includes both FRONTEND_URL env and hardcoded http://localhost:3000 - acceptable for dev/preview but consider env-driven list only for prod
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') - migrate to lifespan context manager (FastAPI >=0.93)

Login response returns token in body AND sets httpOnly cookie - intentional dual fallback per agent note; OK but document that token in body bypasses httpOnly protection if frontend stores in localStorage (XSS exposure)
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login - playbook test_credentials note mentioned 5-fail lockout but not implemented in server.py
Products PUT is full replace via ProductIn (no PATCH) - by design, but means clients must send all fields
seed_data_creation: Seeded automatically by backend startup: 1 admin user + 6 products (Scripts:2, Bases:1, MLOs:2, Vehiculos:1). Test created/deleted TEST_Producto cleanly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core flows verified end-to-end. Backend pytest in /app/backend/tests/test_zemdev_api.py covers auth + products CRUD with create->get->update->get->delete->404 verification. Frontend smoke-tested via Playwright: landing, filters, purchase modal (BBVA visible, Discord button present), admin login (valid+invalid), dashboard create-product modal opens. Brute-force lockout not implemented (was mentioned in playbook prerequisites but not in server.py - flag if user requires).
